entries.d: the first month of the year
entries.s: The new year starts in _____.

entries.d: the second month of the year
entries.s: Valentine's Day is in _____.

entries.d: the third month of the year
entries.s: Spring often begins in _____.

entries.d: the fourth month of the year
entries.s: It often rains a lot in _____.

entries.d: the fifth month of the year
entries.s: The flowers are beautiful in _____.

entries.d: the sixth month of the year
entries.s: Summer holidays start at the end of _____.

entries.d: the seventh month of the year
entries.s: It is very hot here in _____.

entries.d: the eighth month of the year
entries.s: We always go on holiday in _____.

entries.d: the ninth month of the year
entries.s: School starts again in _____.

entries.d: the tenth month of the year
entries.s: Halloween is at the end of _____.

entries.d: the eleventh month of the year
entries.s: The days get very short in _____.

entries.d: the twelfth month of the year
entries.s: Christmas is in _____.
My soft teddy bear is on the pillow of my bed in my _____. That is where I sleep every night.

entries.d: a place with many books to read
entries.s: I go to the quiet _____ with thousands of books on tall shelves to borrow a story book.

entries.d: the room where a family sits together
entries.s: The big soft sofa and the television are in the _____ where we watch films together.

entries.d: the room where you eat meals
entries.s: We eat breakfast together as a family at the big table in the _____ every morning.

entries.d: the room where you wash yourself
entries.s: I brush my teeth and wash my face in the _____ by the sink and mirror every morning.

entries.d: the area inside the front door of a house
entries.s: Please leave your wet shoes and coat in the _____ near the front door when you come into the house.

entries.d: the room where you cook food
entries.s: Mum is making breakfast on the stove in the _____ where the fridge and cooker are.

entries.d: the green area outside your house
entries.s: We grow fresh red tomatoes and colourful flowers in our _____ at the back of the house.

entries.d: a building where you keep your car
entries.s: Dad parks his red car inside the _____ next to the house every evening when he comes home from work.
Monday is the _____ day of the school week.
February is the _____ month of the year.
Wednesday is the _____ day of the school week.
April is the _____ month of the year.
Friday is the _____ day of the school week.
June is the _____ month of the year.
July is the _____ month of the year.
August is the _____ month of the year.
September is the _____ month of the year.
October is the _____ month of the year.
November is the _____ month of the year.
December is the _____ month of the year.
My birthday is on the _____ of May.
Valentine's Day is on the _____ of February.
The test is on the _____ of March.
Her party is on the _____ of April.
The school trip is on the _____ of June.
His birthday is on the _____ of October.
The concert is on the _____ of November.

The party is on the _____ of July.
They arrive on the _____ of August.
The match is on the _____ of January.
Christmas Eve is on the _____ of December.
Christmas Day is on the _____ of December.
The last day of April is the _____.
New Year's Eve is on the _____ of December.

entries.d: a special cake for a birthday
entries.s: Mum made a big chocolate _____ with twelve candles on top for my 12th birthday party.
My little brother is a very slow _____ at the dinner table — it takes him an hour to finish his food.
Tom is _____ today with a high fever. He is staying in bed and his mum is making him soup.
Your bedroom is very _____ with clothes on the floor and toys everywhere. Please tidy it up now!
Can I have a _____ of the chocolate cake, please? Just one slice is enough for me.

entries.d: a place where you watch films
entries.s: Let's go to the _____ with a big screen and popcorn and see the new Marvel film tonight.
You got ten out of ten on the maths test with no mistakes. That is _____ work!
We waited impatiently for a whole hour at the bus stop and _____ the late bus came around the corner.
There is a big football _____ between our team and our rival team on Saturday afternoon at the stadium.

entries.d: a phrase to say today is your special day with cake and presents
entries.s: _____ Today I am eleven years old and we are having a big party with cake!
What is today's _____? — It's March 5th, 2026.

entries.d: one of twelve parts of a year
entries.s: There are exactly twelve _____ in one year, starting with January and ending with December.
_____ — I am eleven. My next birthday is in June.

entries.d: a stick of wax that gives light
entries.s: We put exactly twelve small _____ sticks on top of the birthday cake and lit them all with a match.
This homemade chocolate cake is really _____! I want to eat another big slice right now!
We went on holiday to London _____ summer in July 2025. This summer we are staying home.
The police caught the _____ with a black mask and a bag of stolen money near the bank.

entries.d: the crime of stealing from a place
entries.s: There was a serious _____ at the jewellery shop last night. Thieves took all the gold rings and watches.
I was at my grandma's house _____ — the day before today. Today I am at home.

entries.d: a clock that wakes you up with noise
entries.s: My _____ on the bedside table rings very loudly at seven every morning to wake me up for school.
It is very cloudy and grey outside, so it will _____ rain later this afternoon. I should take my umbrella.

entries.d: saying you are happy for someone
entries.s: You won first prize in the singing competition? _____! I am so happy for your success!

entries.d: a police officer who finds answers
entries.s: The police _____ with a notebook asked the shopkeeper many questions about the robbery to solve the case.

entries.d: showing you are angry at someone
entries.s: You ate my lunch without asking me first! _____ That was very rude!
The speeding car nearly hit us as we crossed the road. _____! We were almost hurt!

entries.g: Nichts zu danken., Keine Ursache., Gern geschehen.
entries.d: what you say after someone thanks you
entries.s: Thank you so much for your help with my homework! — _____. I was happy to help you.
